detaileddescription.para: This documentation provides an overview of the modules directory structure in the AnalysisG project. Each module is designed with specific functionality and interacts with other modules to form the complete system.

sect1.title: Directory Structure
sect1.para: The modules directory contains several subdirectories, each implementing a specific aspect of the system:

sect2.title: structs
sect2.para: The structs module provides fundamental data structures and type definitions used throughout the codebase.

listitem.para: base.cxx: Implements basic type management, translation between ROOT types and internal data enums
include/structs/model.h: Defines model-related structures like model_settings_t
include/structs/enums.h: Contains enum definitions for the project
data_enum: Enumeration for different data types used in the system

bsc_t: Base class for type management with methods like:
root_type_translate: Translates ROOT string types to internal enum types
as_string: Converts internal types to string representation
flush_buffer: Cleans up temporary buffers

model_settings_t: Structure holding model configuration including:
Optimizer settings
Model name, device, checkpoint path
Input/output features for graph, node, and edge components

sect2.title: model
sect2.para: The model module implements the neural network models and related functionality.

listitem.para: model_template.cxx: Implements the base template for models in the system

model_template class: Base class for all models with methods like:
Constructor: Sets up input/output features and device properties
clone(): Creates a copy of the model
register_module(): Registers torch modules with the model
forward(): Processes input graphs (different overloads for single/multiple graphs)
assign_features(): Maps input features to tensors
set_input_features(): Configures input feature mappings

sect2.title: event
sect2.para: The event module handles event data management and processing.

listitem.para: event_template.cxx: Implements base template for event handling

event_template class: Base class for event handling with properties:
trees: Tree collection management
branches: Branch management within trees
leaves: Access to leaf values
name: Event name handling
hash: Event hash identification
tree: Current tree accessor
weight: Event weight handling
index: Event indexing

sect2.title: io
sect2.para: The io module handles input/output operations across the system.

listitem.para: io.cxx: Implements core IO functionality

io class: Handles file operations with methods like:
Constructor/Destructor: Setup and cleanup of IO resources
import_settings(): Imports configuration settings

sect2.title: sampletracer
sect2.para: The sampletracer module tracks and manages samples throughout processing.

listitem.para: sampletracer.cxx: Implements sample tracking functionality

sampletracer class: Manages sample processing with methods like:
compile_objects(): Organizes and processes objects with multi-threading support
Support for progress tracking and visualization
Thread management for parallel processing

sect1.title: Module Interactions
sect1.para: The modules interact in a structured manner:

listitem.para: structs provides base data types used by all other modules
io handles data input/output used by most processing modules
event manages event data that flows into models
model implements neural network functionality using data from events
sampletracer coordinates the processing of multiple samples

sect1.title: Build System
sect1.para: The build system is managed through CMake with the main configuration in CMakeLists.txt. The modules are compiled in a specific order to respect dependencies:

listitem.para: Core utilities (typecasting, notification, structs, tools)
Physics objects (particle, event, selection)
ML components (graph, model, metrics, metric)
Infrastructure (meta, io, plotting, lossfx)
Application level (container, sampletracer, analysis, dataloader, optimizer)

sect1.title: Type System
sect1.para: The codebase implements a custom type system that bridges between ROOT data types and internal representations:

listitem.para: ROOT types like Float_t, Double_t, Int_t are mapped to internal enum values
Vector types are detected and handled separately
Custom string representation for debugging and serialization
